item.title: Why Amazon Has Struggled to Crack India
item.description: More than a decade after entering the country, the company is battling Walmart’s Flipkart and other incumbents.

item.title: Deadly Screwworm Parasite Found in US Threatens Cattle Herd
item.description: A deadly cattle parasite was confirmed in the US for the first time in nearly a decade, posing the latest threat to a US herd already at its lowest level in 75 years.

item.title: Ebola Burial Team Attacked, 11 Patients Flee Care in Widening Outbreak in Congo
item.description: A burial team was attacked and 11 Ebola patients fled isolation facilities in eastern Democratic Republic of Congo, as the outbreak spread to another health zone in the country’s hardest-hit province.

item.title: Honeywell-Backed Quantinuum Raises $1.68 Billion in Upsized IPO
item.description: Quantinuum Inc., a quantum computing company backed by Honeywell International Inc., raised $1.68 billion in its upsized US initial public offering, demonstrating growing investor enthusiasm for the emerging technology.

item.title: Treasury Wine Flags Lower Earnings and Focus on Luxury Market
item.description: Australian vintner Treasury Wine Estates Ltd. is expecting lower earnings for the current financial year as it looks to focus on solidifying key markets and bolstering its position in the luxury portfolio.

item.title: Record Singapore-US Rate Gap May Widen Further on Inflows, Fed
item.description: The gap between Singapore and US swap rates has widened to a record, a trend likely to extend as foreign inflows keep the city state’s liquidity ample while inflationary pressures increase dollar borrowing costs.

item.title: Rupiah Near 18,000 Level Has Markets on Guard for Intervention
item.description: The Indonesian rupiah is closing in on a key psychological level, putting investors on watch for a stronger response from the central bank.

item.title: What to Know About the SpaceX IPO
item.description: Elon Musk’s SpaceX is poised to have the largest stock-market debut in history when it goes public later in June. SpaceX is aiming to raise $75 billion in the initial public offering, more than twice the previous record holder.

item.title: Sam Altman Says He Doesn’t Plan to Put Money Into 2026 Elections
item.description: OpenAI Chief Executive Officer Sam Altman says he has no plans to make any financial contributions toward this year’s US elections, even as other Silicon Valley billionaires have already pledged to spend heavily to influence midterm races that will decide control of Congress.

item.title: Gold Rises as Dip-Buyers Return After Israel-Lebanon Ceasefire
item.description: Gold pushed higher after Israel and Lebanon agreed to a conditional ceasefire, a possible step toward resolving the wider Middle East conflict that’s upended global energy markets and raised inflation risks.

item.title: High Yields Lured UK Money From Cash Funds to Bonds Last Month
item.description: UK-based investors pulled money from cash funds and piled into bonds last month, enticed by surging yields, according to fund flow data compiled by Calastone.

item.title: CFTC Ends Decades-Old ‘Gag Rule’ in Enforcement Settlements
item.description: The Commodity Futures Trading Commission is ending an almost 30-year-old policy that forbade companies and individuals from airing their grievances or denying wrongdoing after they settled enforcement cases with the derivatives regulator.

item.title: McKesson Unit Prices $2.25 Billion Leveraged Loan Ahead of IPO
item.description: A $2.25 billion leveraged loan for McKesson Corp.’s medical-surgical unit priced on Wednesday, ahead of its planned initial public offering.

item.title: Petco Slides as CEO Comments Show Turnaround Not Finished
item.description: Petco Health & Wellness Co. shares fell as much as 18% in postmarket trading after the company’s second-quarter forecast fell below consensus estimates, outweighing a better-than-expected first quarter including a return to positive revenue growth for the first time in nearly two years.

item.title: Argentina’s Dollar Buying Tops $10 Billion Goal on Export Boom
item.description: Less than halfway through the year, Argentina’s central bank has surpassed its $10 billion target for dollar purchases, fueled by a record crop harvest, an energy boom and a surge in debt issuance by provinces and local companies.

item.title: Five Below Slides After Profit Beat as Retailer Flags Consumer Hit Over Oil High Prices
item.description: Shares of Five Below Inc. tumbled on Wednesday after the discount retailer beat first-quarter earnings expectations and raised its full-year profit guidance but signaled caution over the consumer in the months ahead.

item.title: Asian Stocks Set to Fall on Fresh US-Iran Clashes: Markets Wrap
item.description: Asian stocks were poised for losses on Thursday as renewed clashes between the US and Iran added strain to a fragile ceasefire, while pushing oil prices higher.

item.title: Oil Holds Three-Day Gain as Iran-US Clashes Lower Deal Prospects
item.description: Oil slipped after three days of gains as the US and Iran exchanged strikes, threatening the prospects of a peace deal that would reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Is the US Economy Dangerously Dependent on the Rich?
item.description: On this episode of Trumponomics, we discuss the “K-shaped” narrative and whether it’s simply a stand-in for inequality.

item.title: Glazer Family Members Are Studying Manchester United Stake Sale
item.description: Some members of the billionaire Glazer family have been debating whether to sell their stake in Manchester United FC, after more than 20 years of ownership that has often been blighted by fan protest, people familiar with the matter said.

item.title: OpenAI y Anthropic alistan sus OPI que podrían redefinir el mercado de la inteligencia artificial
item.description: Dos grandes de la IA tienen la mirada puesta en los mercados bursátiles, pero algunos se preguntan si la salida a bolsa de SpaceX, prevista para junio, les va a robar el protagonismo.

item.title: Goldman’s Japan Chief Counts on New Leaders to Drive Growth
item.description: Goldman Sachs Japan president Hidehiro Imatsu is promoting younger executives at the firm as a merger boom, rising stocks and volatile bond yields fuel the most intense talent war in years.

item.title: Trade Groups Urge US to Boost Memory Chip Supply Strained by AI
item.description: A global shortage of memory chips fueled by the artificial intelligence boom poses a growing risk to industries ranging from automakers to producers of medical devices, according to a coalition of US business groups who urged the Trump administration to help increase supply of the components.

item.title: SpaceX Seeks $75 Billion in Record IPO Plan to Fund AI, Launch
item.description: SpaceX is seeking to raise $75 billion in an initial public offering that would be the biggest of all time, as Elon Musk’s rocket, space and artificial intelligence company targets a historic debut that could clear a path for more mega-listings.

item.title: USDA Says New World Screwworm Case May Have Been Detected in US
item.description: The US Department of Agriculture said a case of New World screwworm may have been detected in South Texas, a development that could pose a fresh threat to the nation’s cattle herd already at its lowest level in 75 years.

item.title: Broadcom’s Outlook Lets Down Investors Primed for a Blowout
item.description: Broadcom Inc. delivered a disappointing forecast for artificial intelligence chip revenue, signaling that the company is progressing more slowly than anticipated in the burgeoning industry.

item.title: CrowdStrike Shares Drop After Modest Sales Guidance Disappoints
item.description: CrowdStrike Holdings Inc. shares fell after it projected second-quarter revenue that failed to impress investors who had bid up the stock in recent months amid concerns about the threat posed by hackers armed with powerful artificial intelligence tools.

item.title: Logan Says Fed May Need to Raise Interest Rates This Year
item.description: Federal Reserve Bank of Dallas President Lorie Logan said officials may need to raise interest rates later this year to bring inflation back to the US central bank’s 2% target.

item.title: AI CapEx Rush Seen as Continuing as Market Stresses Bubble Up
item.description: The AI-driven boom will ease up at some point in the future but not yet, Diameter Capital Partners co-founder and Managing Partner Scott Goodwin said at the Bloomberg Global Credit Forum on Wednesday.

item.title: AI Euphoria in Old World Auto Stocks Gets a Nod From Wall Street
item.description: Investors betting that more old-world auto companies are in line to benefit from the boom in artificial intelligence got another stamp of approval from Wall Street.
